chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI CR. MISC. APPLN. NO.499 OF 2025 ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Date Order with Signature(s) of Judge(s) ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1 FOR ORDERS ON MA. NO.8182/2025 2. FOR ORDERS ON MA. NO.8183/2025 3. FOR HEARING OF MAIN CASE 4. FOR ORDERS ON MA. NO.8184/2025. 05.06.2025 Mr. Khalid Hussain Khoso, advocate for the applicant ------------------------- 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption application is granted subject to all just exceptions. 3-4. Learned counsel has drawn attention towards application dated 23.04.2025 filed by the SSP Korangi Karachi East wherein it has been clearly mentioned that the cheque was not issued by the present applicant Samiullah. Learned counsel referred to order dated 24.05.2025 and states that even in the said order no connection of the present applicant with the said cheque has been recorded. He then drew attention to pages 41-A and 41-B of the Court file to show that the signature on the subject two cheques are not of the present applicant. Lastly, states that he has no concern whatsoever with the said cheques, or the said firm or with the alleged transaction between respondents No.4 & 5 nor any connection with the Respondents No.4 and 5. Learned counsel lastly pleads that without considering such important factors, directions have been issued against the applicant under Sections 22-A & 22-B, Cr.P.C. for registration of FIR under Section 489-F, PPC which is completely unjustified. Learned counsel has raised serious questions of law and fact which require consideration including the question as to how FIR under Section 489-F, PPC can be registered against a person who has not even issued the cheque?

Learned counsel lastly pleads that without considering such important factors, directions have been issued against the applicant under Sections 22-A & 22-B, Cr.P.C. for registration of FIR under Section 489-F, PPC which is completely unjustified. Learned counsel has raised serious questions of law and fact which require consideration including the question as to how FIR under Section 489-F, PPC can be registered against a person who has not even issued the cheque? Issue notice to the respondents as well as Prosecutor General Sindh for 19.08.2025. Meanwhile, operation of impugned order dated 24.04.2025 only to the extent of applicant Samiullah son of Abdullah is suspended. JUDGE MUSHARRAF ALI
